index.name: Nifty 50 Equal Weight Index
index.description: Assigns equal 2% weight to all 50 Nifty stocks regardless of market cap. Reduces concentration risk from HDFC Bank, Reliance, and ICICI Bank dominating market-cap indices. Historically outperforms cap-weighted Nifty in mid-cycle recoveries and small-cap rallies. Ideal for investors believing in broader market participation.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: Equal weight means Reliance gets 2% vs 10%+ in cap-weighted Nifty. Reduces single-stock concentration. Retail, Jio, and O2C segments driving diversified revenue.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Same 2% as all others vs 13%+ in cap-weighted Nifty. Equal weight reduces excessive banking sector concentration.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Equal 2% weight. Reduces IT sector dominance of market-cap index. Cobalt AI platform and generative AI services driving new revenue streams.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Equal 2% gives same importance as smaller Nifty constituents. Transformation under CEO Bakhshi continues to deliver.

holdings.name: TCS
holdings.description: India's largest IT exporter. Equal weighting reduces exposure to mega-cap IT. AI and cloud services growing share of revenues.

holdings.name: Bajaj Finance
holdings.description: India's largest NBFC. Equal weight elevates Bajaj Finance to same prominence as banking giants. Consumer lending and EMI financing.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: Equal weight elevates L&T vs cap-weighted index. Order book ₹4.7 trillion across infrastructure, defence, and technology segments.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: Equal weight gives pharma same prominence as banks. US specialty products (Ilumya, Cequa, Winlevi) diversifying beyond Indian formulations.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Conservative underwriting culture. Equal weight philosophy aligns with fundamental value approach rather than market-cap momentum.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Post-Citi integration bank with premium credit card franchise. Equal weight treats Axis at same level as larger banking peers.

name: DSP Mutual Fund - DSP Nifty Top 10 Equal Weight ETF

index.name: Nifty Top 10 Equal Weight Index
index.description: DSP Mutual Fund - DSP Nifty Top 10 Equal Weight ETF tracks the Nifty Top 10 Equal Weight Index index.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: In equal-weight index, each Nifty 50 constituent carries the same weight (~2%) regardless of market cap. This reduces concentration in mega-caps like Reliance (which would be 9%+ in cap-weighted). Equal weighting implicitly tilts toward smaller Nifty 50 members.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Equal-weight reduces HDFC Bank from 13-14% (cap-weighted) to ~2%, lowering banking sector concentration. Benefits sectors like metals, pharma and FMCG which have smaller Nifty 50 stocks.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Equal weight approach benefits mid-weight Nifty 50 stocks and reduces large-cap mega-stock dominance. Rebalancing quarterly or semi-annually systematically sells winners and buys underperformers.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: NTPC carries same ~2% weight as HDFC Bank in equal-weight Nifty 50. Value stocks and cyclicals get systematically higher exposure than in cap-weighted — smart-beta tilt.

holdings.name: UltraTech Cement
holdings.description: Equal weight methodology redistributes weight from the 5-6 mega-cap dominators to the remaining 44-45 Nifty 50 companies. Studies show equal-weight outperforms cap-weight over long periods with higher volatility.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: Pharma gets significant equal-weight uplift vs cap-weight. Equal weighting = implicit value/small-ca tilt within large cap universe. Performs better in mean-reversion market environments than momentum-driven markets.

index.name: NIFTY 50 Equal Weight Index

name: Nippon India ETF Nifty 50 Shariah BeES

index.name: Shariah
index.description: Nippon India ETF Nifty 50 Shariah BeES tracks the Shariah index.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Nifty50 Shariah index excludes companies operating in interest-bearing finance, alcohol, tobacco, pork, media and weapons sectors. Infosys is the largest constituent as banking/finance stocks are excluded. Shariah-screened index has a strong IT and pharma tilt.

holdings.name: Tata Consultancy Services
holdings.description: TCS is 2nd largest in Shariah index absent banks. IT companies natural fit for Islamic finance compliance — pure technology services with no interest income. TCS generates 100% of revenue from technology services.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: 3rd largest after IT sector receives overweight in Shariah vs conventional index. Halal-compliant technology business model — revenues from outsourcing, products and engineering services, zero interest income.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: Pharma companies are Shariah-eligible. Sun Pharma is largest pharma company, gains significant weight absent HDFC Bank (~14% in cap-weight) and other banks. Pharmaceutical manufacturing is halal-permissible activity.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: Engineering and construction — Shariah-eligible. L&T's heavy engineering and EPC operations are permissible under Islamic finance principles. Elevated weight vs cap-weight index due to bank exclusion redistributing capital.

holdings.name: Maruti Suzuki
holdings.description: Auto manufacturing is Shariah-compliant. Maruti gains outsized weight in Shariah index. Muslim-majority countries (Gulf, Indonesia, Malaysia) are growing export markets for Maruti — strategic fit between index and business geography.
